message:
feat(tui): expand TUI interactive views and rotate beads history

Extend tui.rs (+209 lines) with improved interactive table rendering,
detail panel navigation, and scroll state management. Rotate stale
beads history snapshots and sync tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","title":"Define the evidence standard for parity claims so beads can be closed honestly","description":"Purpose:
Define what counts as sufficient evidence for saying a surface is done. Some parity claims can be established by fixture-backed conformance; others need TUI journey coverage, shell e2e, or targeted regression tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Different evidence classes are spelled out for robot, TUI, export/pages, and workspace/hook surfaces.
- The plan distinguishes proof of implementation from proof of behavioral equivalence.
- Closing guidance is strict enough that we do not recreate the current overclaim problem.","notes":"Parity evidence standard:

A. General rule
- A parity claim is valid only when the tracker can point to both the implementation anchor and the proof anchor.
- "Implemented" is not enough. We distinguish: implemented, proven equivalent, partially proven, divergent-by-design, and unknown.
- If the behavior affects operator recovery, path resolution, or cross-mode/context continuity, pure happy-path testing is insufficient.

B. Evidence ladder for loader/model/path semantics
- Required minimum: focused unit tests for parsing/validation rules plus CLI-entry integration coverage for cwd/repo/workspace/beads-file resolution semantics.
- Needed when legacy behavior is subtle: adversarial fixtures or integration cases that exercise malformed input, missing files, ambiguous roots, and precedence rules.
- Closure bar: a future agent can tell which root/path semantics are guaranteed and which are merely inferred.

C. Evidence ladder for robot/CLI contract parity
- Required minimum: one deterministic proof layer near the implementation (unit/conformance/schema) and one proof layer from the real CLI entrypoint.
- Use conformance fixtures when legacy output shape/order/omission is the source of truth.
- Use schema validation when shape contracts matter beyond a single fixture.
- Use CLI integration/e2e when path/config resolution, error behavior, or multi-flag interaction matters.
- Closure bar: command family behavior, default behavior, filter behavior, and failure behavior are all evidenced at the right layer.

D. Evidence ladder for TUI workflow parity
- Required minimum: state/keyflow coverage for the interaction logic plus either snapshot or journey evidence for the user-visible workflow.
- Use snapshots when layout/detail visibility matters.
- Use keyflow/state tests when search/filter/focus/selection transitions matter.
- Use multi-mode journey tests when the operator workflow crosses views or depends on sustained context.
- Closure bar: we can explain not just that a key exists, but that the operator can complete the legacy job-to-be-done.

E. Evidence ladder for export/pages/workspace integration parity
- Required minimum: targeted unit/integration coverage for root-cause semantics plus shell/e2e proof for operator workflows with detailed logs.
- Required logging: cwd/repo/workspace context, replay command, stdout/stderr, and any preview/watch lifecycle artifacts relevant to diagnosis.
- Use failure-path cases deliberately: invalid saved config, broken hook, non-repo cwd, watch reload edge cases, preview path traversal protections, and deploy-target repair flows.
- Closure bar: both happy path and operator recovery path are evidenced.

F. Documentation truthfulness standard
- Docs may only claim parity at the strongest evidence level actually achieved.
- Additive Rust-only features must be labeled as additive, not counted as proof that a legacy workflow is covered.
- Intentional divergences must name the preserved user outcome and why the difference is acceptable.

G. Anti-overclaim rule
- A bead cannot close by citing only old markdown prose or broad suite counts. It must name the specific tests, fixtures, scripts, or artifacts that establish confidence for that surface.
- If evidence is missing but the code looks plausible, classify as partial or unknown until proof exists.

H. Diagnostic quality standard
- Tests and scripts used as parity evidence must fail loudly and locally. A future agent should be able to infer the broken workflow, command family, mode, path context, or fixture from the failure output without rereading the implementation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.803904168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:29.806036575Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:29.805311652Z","close_reason":"Tracker now records the parity evidence standard, including required proof ladders and the anti-overclaim rule for closing beads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.803904168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":135,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Evidence standard recorded:
Parity closure now requires paired implementation and proof anchors, with explicit ladders for loader/path semantics, robot CLI contracts, TUI workflows, export/pages/workspace integrations, and documentation truthfulness. The anti-overclaim rule is explicit: no bead closes on broad suite counts or optimistic prose alone.","created_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","title":"Record the fresh gap ledger with explicit statuses: proven, partial, unknown, divergent-by-design","description":"Purpose:
Translate the inventory and evidence standard into an actionable gap ledger. This is the gate between planning and implementation.

Requirements:
- Every material gap becomes a child bead in one of the execution tracks.
- Every currently claimed-complete surface is either revalidated or downgraded.
- Any intentional divergence must include why it is acceptable and what user outcome remains covered.","notes":"Fresh gap ledger snapshot:

1. Loader/model/root-resolution semantics
- Status: partial / high-risk.
- Why: recent real bugs in feedback storage, hook cwd, preview path handling, wizard repair routing, and repo-path/beads-file inference all came from this family.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.2.1`, `bd-2bpl.2.3.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.3`, `bd-2bpl.4.4.3`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.2`.

2. Core robot command families (triage/next/plan/priority/insights/diff/history/forecast/capacity/burndown/suggest/graph)
- Status: implemented and broadly proven, but not yet re-audited under the new evidence standard. Treat as provisionally partial until command-by-command audit confirms defaults, filters, omission rules, and failure behavior.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.2.2`, `bd-2bpl.2.3.2`, `bd-2bpl.2.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.2.4.2`.

3. Extended/additive robot surfaces (search, label intel, recipes, docs/schema, metrics, feedback, drift, correlation/impact, file-intel, briefs, sprint helpers)
- Status: mixed. Some are additive Rust improvements rather than strict legacy surfaces; others are claimed complete in docs but need truthfulness review so additive does not get confused with parity proof.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.2.2`, `bd-2bpl.5.1`.

4. Main TUI workflow parity
- Status: partial / unproven at operator-workflow level. Existing snapshots and keybindings are not enough by themselves to prove users can complete the same browsing, prioritization, and detail-inspection tasks as in legacy `bv`.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.3.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.2`.

5. Board/insights/graph TUI workflow parity
- Status: partial / unproven at workflow level. The modes are rich and heavily tested, but the new standard requires proof of operator jobs-to-be-done rather than mode existence or keybinding coverage.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.3.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.4.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.2`.

6. History-mode and cross-mode investigative parity
- Status: partial / high-value audit target. History is specifically vulnerable to overclaim because individual features can exist without the full investigative loop feeling complete.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.3.3`, `bd-2bpl.3.4.3`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.2`.

7. Export/pages/preview/watch/wizard workflows
- Status: implemented and actively tested, but still provisional under the new standard because happy-path coverage and feature presence do not automatically prove workflow equivalence or recovery-path clarity.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.2`, `bd-2bpl.4.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.4.2`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.2`.

8. Documentation and release-readiness claims
- Status: incorrect/overstated in current form. README and FEATURE_PARITY contain strong completion language that now must be treated as claims to be re-earned, not evidence.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.5.2`.

9. Divergent-by-design/additive surfaces
- Status: divergent-by-design where clearly Rust-specific and not required for legacy parity, but must be labeled honestly. Examples: Rust-only TUI modes, schema/docs outputs, some metrics/reporting differences.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.5.1` plus whatever audit bead owns the adjacent workflow family.

Planning conclusion
- No additional top-level tracks are needed right now; the current `bd-2bpl.2` through `.5` split is sufficient.
- The first implementation-phase priorities should stay audit-first: `bd-2bpl.2.1`, `bd-2bpl.2.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.3`, `bd-2bpl.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.2`, and `bd-2bpl.4.3`.
- The strongest current downgrade is this: broad implementation exists, but exact capability parity is not yet proven under the new evidence standard.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.016828423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:08.151000528Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:08.150434155Z","close_reason":"Fresh gap ledger recorded in tracker notes/comments, with provisional statuses and explicit mapping from each gap family to the current execution beads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","gaps","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.016828423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:21.706308818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:22.959815646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":136,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Gap ledger recorded:
The tracker now explicitly downgrades several surfaces from implicit "done" to provisional partial status until fresh audit evidence exists. The biggest current risk classes are path/root-resolution semantics, operator-workflow proof for the TUI, export/pages recovery-path behavior, and documentation truthfulness. No new top-level tracks are needed; the current execution tree is sufficient.","created_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2","title":"Audit and close robot/CLI parity gaps against legacy bv semantics","description":"Background:
Robot-mode parity is the most objective and automation-critical surface. Even small path-resolution, filtering, metadata-shape, or loader-semantic mismatches can break downstream agent workflows.

Goal:
Systematically re-audit all legacy robot and CLI behaviors against the Rust port, then fix any remaining gaps in semantics, outputs, or workflow compatibility.

Acceptance:
- Loader, model, and path-resolution semantics are rechecked against legacy behavior.
- Every legacy robot command family has explicit parity evidence or a documented intentional divergence.
- Integration and conformance coverage exists for all reopened gaps.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.802710388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.077749232Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.802710388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Why this matters:

Done means:
There is a concrete list of matching semantics, mismatches, and required fixes/tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:41:49.475084253Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["loader","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:24.264893609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":137,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: audited loader and path-resolution semantics against legacy worktree expectations and found a real parity gap in .beads discovery for linked git worktrees. Root cause: get_beads_dir() only searched ancestor .beads directories and did not fall back from a worktree .git pointer to the main repo .beads; the first implementation of that fallback also needed canonicalization of commondir to resolve dot-dot segments correctly. Fix landed in src/loader.rs: parse worktree .git, resolve gitdir, canonicalize commondir, derive the main repo root, and use the main-repo .beads when present. Added regression test get_beads_dir_falls_back_to_main_repo_for_git_worktree. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_falls_back_to_main_repo_for_git_worktree passed; prior rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets and rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets on this slice were green. cargo fmt --check remains red repo-wide due existing unrelated drift in other files, but src/loader.rs was formatted with rustfmt --edition 2024.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:24:04Z"},{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: hardened the linked-worktree fallback in src/loader.rs after review identified an ancestor-scan correctness hole. Root cause: find_worktree_main_repo_beads_dir_from() used early-return option propagation inside the ancestor loop, so any malformed or non-worktree .git file in an intermediate ancestor aborted the entire scan before reaching the real worktree root. Fix: treat each ancestor independently, continue past invalid .git pointers or unusable commondir data, and only return when a valid main-repo .beads directory is found. Added regression test get_beads_dir_ignores_malformed_intermediate_git_file_during_worktree_fallback alongside the existing worktree fallback tests. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_ passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails on unrelated pre-existing drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:42:58Z"},{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: the audit found a model-level validation gap in src/model.rs. Legacy validation requires updated_at to be greater than or equal to created_at when both timestamps are present, but Issue::validate() was not checking that invariant at all. Fix: reject issues whose updated_at is earlier than created_at while still accepting equal timestamps. Added direct unit regressions validate_rejects_updated_at_before_created_at and validate_accepts_equal_created_and_updated_timestamps. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib validate_rejects_updated_at_before_created_at passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib validate_accepts_equal_created_and_updated_timestamps passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails only because of unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T20:47:34Z"},{"id":140,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: found and fixed a CLI-to-loader parity mismatch around warning suppression. Root cause: loader warning suppression only consulted BV_ROBOT=1, but normal bvr --robot-* invocations did not set that environment variable, so malformed-line and invalid-issue warnings could still leak to stderr in ordinary robot mode. Fix: added loader-level robot-warning suppression state, wired main.rs to enable it whenever Cli::is_robot_command() is true before issue loading, and preserved the existing BV_ROBOT env behavior as an additional trigger. Added CLI integration regression robot_triage_suppresses_loader_warnings_without_bv_robot_env to prove malformed JSON warnings stay suppressed during normal --robot-triage usage. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_model_validation robot_triage_suppresses_loader_warnings_without_bv_robot_env passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails only because of unrelated pre-existing drift outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:36:08Z"},{"id":141,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Audit continuation (PearlMill): current loader/model/path code still covers deterministic JSONL selection, first-line BOM stripping, malformed-line suppression in robot mode, workspace discovery/namespacing, linked-worktree .beads fallback, and updated_at >= created_at validation. The `br ready` vs `bvr --robot-next` disagreement reproduced in this session is not a loader/path source-of-truth bug in this repo: `.beads/` currently only contains `issues.jsonl`. The immediate cause is triage-layer behavior in `src/analysis/triage.rs`, which excludes `in_progress` issues from recommendations, while `br ready` still lists the current P0 audit beads despite their `in_progress` status. That belongs under the robot-command audit/fix track (`bd-2bpl.2.2` / `bd-2bpl.2.3.2`), not as a new loader/model/path mismatch. Verification this session: `rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_` passed, `rch exec -- cargo test --lib parse_issues_from_text_strips_bom` passed, and `rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_model_validation diff_since_auto_robot_diff_suppresses_loader_warnings -- --exact` passed.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:41:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","title":"Re-audit each robot command family for behavioral and output-shape equivalence","description":"Purpose:
Review every legacy robot family again with fresh eyes: triage, next, plan, insights, priority, diff, history, forecast, capacity, burndown, suggest, graph, search, label intelligence, recipes/scripts, docs/schema, and admin or operational flags.

Focus:
- Field shape and omission rules.

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","title":"Rebuild the bvr TUI around the full FrankenTUI showcase contract before claiming parity","description":"Background:
The current TUI audit showed that `bvr` is still architecturally closer to a text-report renderer than to a flagship operator console. Most screens are composed from large multiline strings dropped into generic bordered paragraphs, while the repo documentation and parity ledger still imply that the TUI is largely complete. That mismatch is now itself a project risk.

Why this bead exists:
This epic is the canonical redesign track for turning the audit into executable work. The core requirement is not merely to make the Rust TUI prettier. It is to deliberately exploit the advanced capabilities already available in FrankenTUI/`ftui` so that `bvr` first recovers the workflow confidence and visual richness of legacy `bv`, and only then goes beyond it.

Non-negotiable design intent:
- Use every relevant high-end FrankenTUI capability that materially improves parity and operator effectiveness: global shell chrome, theme cycling, responsive layout tiers, pane geometry, hit-tested interaction regions, richer search affordances, semantic panels, stronger signal density, grapheme-safe text handling, terminal hyperlinks, and layout/visual diagnostics.
- Do not confuse a stable snapshot with a good product. The redesign must optimize for scanability, confidence, density, discoverability, and mode identity.
- Do not simplify away legacy workflows. Better-than-legacy improvements are welcome only after the legacy job-to-be-done is actually covered.

Scope of this epic:
1. Reset the TUI parity contract so the tracker and docs no longer overstate reality.
2. Build the missing shell, layout, visual, and interaction infrastructure required for a serious FrankenTUI implementation.
3. Rebuild the flagship main screen, then the board, graph, insights, and history surfaces around that infrastructure.
4. Add redesign-specific diagnostics and proof harnesses so future maintainers can validate quality without relying on tribal memory.

Acceptance:
- The child beads are sufficiently detailed that an implementer does not need to reopen `TUI_GAP_ANALYSIS.md` or prior planning docs to know what success means.
- The resulting implementation path is explicitly ordered: contract reset -> infrastructure -> flagship main screen -> secondary modes -> proof/evidence.
- Docs/verdict beads later in the tree depend on this redesign evidence instead of restating optimistic claims.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:39.972459177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:39.972459177Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","parity","redesign","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:39.972459177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Add the text-measurement and terminal-native interaction foundations that a flagship TUI needs before we scale up the redesign.

Background:
The current TUI is string-heavy. That makes it vulnerable to Unicode width bugs, truncation mistakes, and inconsistent wrapping as screens become richer. It also leaves terminal-native affordances like OSC-8 hyperlinks underused even though `bvr` has many natural link targets: issue IDs, repos, commits, docs, and external references.

Required scope:
- Adopt grapheme-safe, display-width-aware layout and truncation discipline for screen rendering.
- Add richer wrapping/truncation helpers appropriate for dense panels and narrow layouts.
- Introduce hyperlink handling for issue/detail/history/graph contexts where terminal links materially improve usability.
- Make links keyboard- and mouse-accessible rather than treating them as dead text.

Why it belongs early:
- It is easier to get width correctness and link affordances right before all screens are rebuilt.
- History and graph especially will benefit from terminal-native navigation once they stop being plain reports.

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","title":"Rebuild the flagship main triage surface as a cockpit-grade operator view","description":"Purpose:
Turn the default screen into the flagship experience that proves the redesign is real. This is the screen that must immediately feel like a serious triage console rather than a formatted report.

Background:
The audit found that the main screen contains useful data, but its scan line is too thin, its detail pane is too report-like, and its discoverability is overly dependent on footer prose. Because this is the entry screen, that weakness shapes the entire first impression of `bvr`.

Required scope:
- Redesign the list rows to surface far more decision-critical information at a glance.
- Recompose the detail pane into strong modules with explicit visual hierarchy and action orientation.
- Upgrade main-screen search, filter, focus, and discovery affordances so the screen is fast in the first 3 seconds, not only after a user learns the keymap.

Design intent:
- This screen should establish the visual and interaction patterns that secondary modes then inherit or specialize.
- Better-than-legacy improvements are allowed, but only if the legacy decision loop becomes faster and clearer, not more ornamental.

Acceptance:
- A user can decide what to inspect or act on with far less scanning effort than in the current build.
- The screen reads as a cockpit with modules, not a single large report.
- Search/filter/focus behavior is visibly integrated into the product rather than hidden in text hints.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. This is the flagship bead. The rebuilt main view must use the shell, layout, and visual primitives from earlier beads rather than bypassing them.\n3. Every major interaction improvement should be encoded into deterministic tests so the flagship screen does not regress later.\n\nFuture-self note: If this bead lands and still feels like a report viewer, the redesign is not actually working. This is the screen that must prove the new architecture pays off.","acceptance_criteria":"1.  {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","title":"Redesign main-screen rows with dense scan-line chips, rank signals, blocker context, and stronger information scent","description":"Purpose:
Make the main list itself do real triage work before the operator ever opens the detail pane.

Background:
Today the main rows mostly expose ID, status, priority, and title. That leaves the operator blind to many of the cues that actually drive prioritization: blocker load, unblocks count, age/staleness, assignee, repo, urgency, and rank/score context.

Required scope:
- Replace the current low-information row format with a compact but legible scan line built from semantic chips/badges and aligned signals.
- Surface enough context that a user can compare items without repeatedly opening the detail pane.
- Preserve readability at narrow widths by using responsive row variants rather than just truncating everything.

Acceptance:
- Main-list rows communicate materially more useful triage information than the current implementation.
- Width-specific row treatments exist so the redesign holds up across sizes.
- The result improves scanability without becoming visually noisy.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Treat each row as a triage decision surface, not a decorative summary line.\n2. Encode rank, blocker load, ownership, freshness, and scope cues so users can compare items without opening the detail pane repeatedly.\n3. Define distinct row behavior for crowded, filtered, and no-hit contexts so the list still teaches the operator what happened.\n4. Keep the row system stable under long titles, long labels, Unicode, and dense datasets by relying on the shared text-width and visual-primitives work.\n\nFuture-self note: If the rows only look denser in screenshots but still force constant detail-pane opening, this bead missed the point.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Main-list rows communicate materially more useful triage information than the current implementation.\n2. Width-specific row treatments exist so the redesign holds up across sizes, dense datasets, and long/Unicode content.\n3. Filtered, empty, and no-hit list states remain legible and recovery-oriented rather than collapsing into dead space.\n4. Unit, snapshot, and keyflow coverage prove row behavior for dense datasets, filtered states, and degraded-width variants.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.090883026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:24:38.443227999Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["density","frankentui","main-view","triage","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:50.454890035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:50.812307267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.090883026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","title":"Recompose the main detail pane into a modular cockpit: summary, action state, dependencies, metrics, and narrative","description":"Purpose:
Replace the current line-oriented report in the main detail pane with a modular, high-hierarchy layout that makes actionability and context obvious.

Why this matters:
The current detail pane has valuable data, but it is arranged like a narrative dump. Operators should be able to understand state, blockers, risk, and recommended action from a small number of visually distinct modules.

Required scope:
- Create a high-priority summary module that immediately answers: what is this, why does it matter, and what should I do next?
- Separate action state, dependencies, graph signals, and narrative/body content into visually distinct groups.
- Use semantic panels, badges, metric strips, and hierarchy rather than relying on whitespace and colons alone.
- Keep rich long-form fields readable without letting them dominate the top of the pane.

Acceptance:
- The top half of the detail pane communicates the core state/action story much faster than the current design.
- Rich narrative sections still exist, but they no longer crowd out the operational summary.
- The detail pane feels like a decision cockpit rather than a report body.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Make dependency, risk, and action cues scannable enough that the pane helps with prioritization rather than only documentation.\n3. Handle sparse metadata and overlong narrative gracefully so the pane still feels intentional when fields are missing or huge.\n4. Preserve drill links and adjacent-workflow handoffs where they improve operator flow.\n\nFuture-self note: This pane should still be useful when data is messy, incomplete, or verbose. If it only looks good on perfect issues, it is not done.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The top half of the detail pane communicates the core state/action story much faster than the current design.\n2. Unit, snapshot, and journey/keyflow coverage prove both normal and degraded detail-pane states, including long content and linked handoffs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.387066833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:24:45.671442172Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["detail","frankentui","main-view","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:51.180090767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:51.562731182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.387066833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
